PROJECT 01 · CURRENT

Marine Power Optimisation & Virtual Twin

MarineSitu  ·  DOE / ORISE / WPTO Marine Energy Fellow  ·  2025—Present

Current

Problem

Plan and validate multi-day duty-cycle schedules for solar-powered, off-grid underwater monitoring platforms running cameras, sonar, UV-C antifouling, and acoustic sensors on battery alone.

Approach

Built a Python virtual twin coupling solar irradiance (ERA5 reanalysis), battery state-of-charge, and per-sensor power draw across multi-day deployments. Wrote three solvers (greedy heuristic, MILP, and LP) that produce optimised sampling schedules under battery, duty-cycle, and safety-floor constraints. Validated against ERA5 solar data across five candidate deployment sites from Hawaii to Anchorage.

Result

Tool produces deployable sampling schedules and identifies which deployment scenarios are feasible at each site and season, directly informing field deployment planning.

PythonSciPy CVXPYMILP LPERA5 reanalysis Battery modellingNVIDIA Jetson Edge computing

PROJECT 02

Multi-Component Alloy Development & Thermal Qualification

Zap Energy, Advanced Materials Group  ·  2024

2024

Developed and qualified multi-component metal alloy systems and porous refractory-metal structures for extreme-environment thermal management hardware supporting fusion-energy component qualification.

Thermophysical Property Evaluation — Liquid Metal Blanket Materials

  • Prepared research alloys in an inert atmosphere glovebox.
  • Investigated eutectic composition of a novel alloy family via thermal analysis with a Netzsch STA/TGA-DSC.
  • Evaluated alloy microstructure using SEM/EDX and optical microscopy.

Porous Tungsten Structure Characterisation

  • Conducted COMSOL simulations to evaluate structure porosity and permeability.
  • Measured structure density by Archimedes' method.
Thermodynamic modellingTernary eutectic alloys STA (TGA/DSC)SEM/EDX Glovebox opsVacuum ops COMSOL porous mediaM&P workflows

PROJECT 03

Heat Transfer & Evaporation Studies

UCSD Thermal Energy Materials & Physics (TEMP) Lab  ·  2023

2023

Work Performed

  • Performed evaporation experiments on AAO, glass fibre, and hydrogels — analysing capillary pressure, permeability, and thin-film spreading to optimise heat transfer efficiency in high-performance cooling applications.
  • Troubleshot and resolved experimental issues including water transport inconsistencies, CHF deviations, and multimeter inaccuracies, ensuring precise data collection and system stability.
  • Applied fluid mechanics principles (Young-Laplace equation and Darcy's Law), demonstrating that optimised materials could sustain heat loads up to 600 W while mitigating scaling and evaporation inefficiencies.

Key Result

CHF characterised up to 600 W/cm² on GB-140 samples. Directly informed material selection for high-performance thermal management.

Capillary flowAAO & hydrogel wicks Young-LaplaceDarcy's Law CHF characterisationPython DAQ Root-cause troubleshooting

Related reading

Feng, T., Pei, Y., Zhang, H., Asai, B., Dong, G., Joshi, A., Saha, A., Cai, S., & Chen, R. — High-flux and stable thin-film evaporation from fiber membranes with interconnected pores. Joule 9(7), 101975 (2025). Glass-fiber-membrane capillary evaporator from the UCSD Chen group reaching ~486 W/cm² at ΔT ≈ 7 °C — thin-film evaporation aimed at data-center / AI chip cooling.

PROJECT 04

Falling-Film Evaporation & Pool Boiling Research

UIUC Energy & Multiphase Flow Lab  ·  2021–2022

2021–22

Saltwater Falling-Film Evaporation & Anti-Fouling · 2022

  • Conducted falling-film evaporation experiments on stainless steel and 3D-printed polymer tubes, evaluating surface coatings, wettability gradients, and groove patterns on scaling behaviour and heat transfer efficiency.
  • Performed image processing and wave-velocity analysis in Python (OpenCV, Pandas) to quantify fouling-layer growth, liquid retention, and flow distribution.
  • Assisted in CFD model validation, manuscript editing, and data analysis, integrating heat- and mass-transfer principles to assess crystallisation fouling mechanisms in thermal desalination.
  • Contributed to scanning electron microscopy (SEM) characterisation of CaCO₃ crystal morphology, comparing dense scale layers on hydrophilic regions against scattered deposition on hydrophobic bands and the distinct thickness step at wettability boundaries.

Pool Boiling & Bubble Nucleation · 2021

  • Studied bubble-nucleation dynamics by testing stainless steel samples with varying micron-scale groove densities to analyse heat transfer performance.
  • Conducted pool-boiling experiments using an automated lab test system with DAQ, heater control, and high-speed imaging for data collection.
  • Analysed nucleation-site distribution and thermal performance to assess material surface effects on boiling.

Publications

Co-authored ASME J. Heat & Mass Transfer (2024) & Desalination (2023).

Pool boilingFalling-film evaporation Anti-fouling surfacesOpenCV Python DAQAnsys Fluent High-speed imaging

PROJECT 05 · PERSONAL

Transient 2D Heat Conduction Solver

Personal project  ·  Python / NumPy / Matplotlib

Personal

Problem

Build a numerical model of an electronic chip dissipating heat into a 2D substrate with mixed boundary conditions — foundational for spacecraft electronics thermal control.

Approach & Result

Implemented an explicit finite-difference (FTCS) scheme for the 2D transient heat equation on a 20×20 grid. Embedded a constant-temperature heat source; applied Dirichlet BCs on three sides and a convective (Robin) BC on the fourth. Built in a Fourier-number stability check and vectorised the inner loop with NumPy. Recovers the expected thermal field: heat-source spike, edge gradients, and convective cooling on the open boundary.

PythonNumPy MatplotlibFDM / FTCS Transient conductionStability analysis
